name: etcd
description: Distributed reliable key-value store for the most critical data of a distributed system, powering Kubernetes and other cloud-native infrastructure

hosting-options.comment: Self-hosted is primary model. IBM Cloud offers Databases for etcd as managed service. No official managed offering from etcd team.

data-model.comment: Flat key-value model with no native hierarchical or nested data structures

topology.comment: Uses Raft consensus algorithm with single leader for writes, followers replicate

sharding.comment: etcd does not support automatic sharding. Data is replicated across all nodes, not partitioned. For very large datasets, users must manually partition data across multiple etcd clusters.

replication-factor.comment: Recommended cluster sizes of 3, 5, or 7 nodes for fault tolerance. All data replicated to all nodes.

multi-region.comment: Not recommended for multi-region due to latency sensitivity. Documentation recommends deploying within a single data center.

cap-position.comment: Prioritizes consistency over availability. Uses Raft consensus requiring majority quorum for writes.

consistency-model.comment: Provides strict serializability (linearizable + serializable) for all operations. Jepsen confirmed this in v3.4.3 testing.

acid-transactions.comment: Supports multi-key transactions with compare-and-swap operations. Transactions are atomic.

transaction-scope.comment: Transactions can span any keys in the cluster. All data is on all nodes.

isolation-levels.comment: Strict serializable isolation for all operations

distributed-transactions.comment: Uses Raft consensus for coordinating writes across nodes

query-language.comment: gRPC API only. No query language - just key-value operations (get, put, delete, range queries, watch)

secondary-indexes.comment: No secondary indexes. Keys are the only index.

joins.comment: Key-value store with no join capabilities

aggregations.comment: No aggregation support. Application must perform aggregations.

max-cluster-size.comment: Recommended max of 7 nodes. Larger clusters increase latency due to Raft consensus overhead. Designed for small metadata stores, not large data volumes.

write-throughput.comment: Official benchmark figure on SSD storage

read-latency-p99.comment: No official p99 latency figures published. Latency depends heavily on disk I/O.

auto-scaling.comment: No automatic scaling. Cluster membership changes require manual intervention.

hot-spot-handling.comment: All data replicated to all nodes, so hot spots are handled by the entire cluster. No automatic rebalancing mechanism.

zero-downtime-upgrades.comment: Rolling upgrades supported - upgrade nodes one at a time while cluster continues serving requests

online-schema-changes.comment: Not applicable - etcd is a schema-less key-value store

point-in-time-recovery.comment: Snapshot-based recovery. Can restore to any snapshot point. The --bump-revision option allows proper revision handling after restore.

backup-strategy.comment: Built-in snapshot functionality via etcdctl snapshot save. Snapshots are point-in-time backups.

kubernetes-operator.comment: Official operator at github.com/etcd-io/etcd-operator. Also community operators available.

go-driver.comment: Official etcd/client/v3 maintained by etcd team

java-driver.comment: coreos/jetcd is the official Java client supporting v3 API

python-driver.comment: kragniz/python-etcd3 and Revolution1/etcd3-py for v3 API. Not officially maintained by etcd team.

c-cpp-driver.comment: etcd-cpp-apiv3/etcd-cpp-apiv3 for v3, multiple community C libraries for v2

orm-support.comment: Not applicable - etcd is a key-value store, not a relational database

free-tier.comment: IBM Cloud Databases for etcd offers a free tier. Compose offered 30-day free trial.

pricing-model.comment: IBM Cloud pricing based on RAM, storage, and vCPUs allocated

cost-estimate-small.comment: Small deployment on IBM Cloud Databases for etcd. Self-hosted is free (infrastructure costs only).

change-data-capture.comment: No built-in CDC. The Watch API provides real-time notifications but isn't CDC in the traditional sense.

watch-subscribe.comment: Excellent Watch API - clients can subscribe to key changes with ordered, unique, reliable, atomic, and resumable guarantees. Core feature used by Kubernetes.

triggers.comment: No server-side triggers. Watch API is client-side subscription model.

event-streaming.comment: Custom gRPC streaming via Watch API. No native Kafka/Pulsar integration - requires external tooling.

stackoverflow-tags.comment: Unable to retrieve exact count. etcd questions exist on Stack Overflow under the 'etcd' tag.
